If a decision wouldn't be acceptable for my own life, it doesn't belong in the product."* > > **- Travis J. Neuman** **[Read the full story in the app](https://brain.neuman.dev)** (Click "About" in the footer) --- ## Features ### Notes & Knowledge Management **Status:** Shipped (55% Obsidian parity, 50% Notion parity) | Feature | Status | Description | |---------|--------|-------------| | Rich Text Editor | ✅ Shipped | Lexical-powered editor with live preview | | Markdown Mode | ✅ Shipped | Full markdown editing support | | Folder Hierarchy | ✅ Shipped | Unlimited depth, drag-and-drop organization | | Full-Text Search | ✅ Shipped | Cmd/Ctrl+K quick switcher | | Auto-Save | ✅ Shipped | Debounced saves to IndexedDB | | Slash Commands | ✅ Shipped | /heading, /list, /code, /quote, /table | | Export to Markdown | ✅ Shipped | Download notes as .md files | | Word/Character Count | ✅ Shipped | Live status bar updates | | [[Wiki-links]] | 📋 Planned | Coming Q2 2026 | | Graph View | 💡 Future | Visual knowledge connections | --- ### Task & Project Management **Status:** Shipped (98% Jira/Linear parity) | Feature | Status | Description | |---------|--------|-------------| | Kanban Board | ✅ Shipped | Drag-and-drop tasks across columns | | Custom Columns | ✅ Shipped | Fully customizable workflow stages | | Subtasks | ✅ Shipped | Nested tasks with full properties | | Task Dependencies | ✅ Shipped | "Blocked By" and "Blocks" relationships | | List View | ✅ Shipped | Sortable columns, bulk operations | | Due Dates | ✅ Shipped | Calendar integration | | Priority Levels | ✅ Shipped | High/Medium/Low with visual indicators | | Export to CSV | ✅ Shipped | Full data export | | Gantt Charts | 📋 Planned | Timeline visualization | **Key Capabilities:** - Circular dependency prevention - Completion tracking with progress bars - Three-color badge system (green/blue/gray) - Bulk select, status change, priority change, delete - Card detail panel with rich editing --- ### Time Tracking **Status:** Shipped (74% Toggl parity) | Feature | Status | Description | |---------|--------|-------------| | Timer Controls | ✅ Shipped | Start/stop/pause/resume | | Sidebar Panel | ✅ Shipped | Toggl-style persistent timer | | **Timer Tab** | ✅ Shipped | Full-featured Schedule page tab | | Recent Entries | ✅ Shipped | 48-hour history with continue | | Manual Entry | ✅ Shipped | Add retroactive time entries | | Project Support | ✅ Shipped | Create, assign, filter by project | | Daily/Weekly Stats | ✅ Shipped | Visual charts and summaries | | Monthly Reports | ✅ Shipped | Heat map, project breakdown | | Calendar View | ✅ Shipped | Month grid, color-coded by project | | CSV Export | ✅ Shipped | RFC 4180 compliant | | Bulk Operations | ✅ Shipped | Multi-select, delete, update | | Billable Hours | 📋 Planned | Coming Phase 5 | **Timer Tab Features (New!):** - Synchronized with sidebar timer - Weekly statistics chart with daily breakdown - Today/weekly/daily average displays - Recent entries grid with click-to-continue --- ### Calendar & Scheduling **Status:** Shipped (65% Google Calendar parity) | Feature | Status | Description | |---------|--------|-------------| | Month/Week/Day Views | ✅ Shipped | Full calendar navigation | | Event Creation/Editing | ✅ Shipped | Rich event details | | Drag-and-Drop | ✅ Shipped | Reschedule events easily | | Recurring Events | ✅ Shipped | Daily, weekly, monthly patterns | | Multi-Day Events | ✅ Shipped | Visual representation | | Event Reminders | ✅ Shipped | Browser notifications | | ICS Import/Export | ✅ Shipped | Google Calendar compatible | | Agenda View | ✅ Shipped | Next 30 days list | | Conflict Detection | ✅ Shipped | Overlapping events warning | | Task Integration | ✅ Shipped | Due dates on calendar | | Day Detail Modal | ✅ Shipped | Click any day for full view | --- ### Dashboard & Widgets **Status:** Shipped (70% Notion Dashboard parity) **44+ widgets across 5 categories:** | Category | Widgets | Examples | |----------|---------|----------| | **Productivity** | 6 | TaskSummary, UpcomingEvents, RecentNotes, QuickAdd, Pomodoro | | **Information** | 9 | Weather, HackerNews, Reddit, DevTo, GitHub, Quotes | | **Utilities** | 14 | Calculator, QRCode, ColorPalette, Countdown, WorldClock | | **Finance** | 3 | Crypto, Currency, StockMarket | | **Media** | 1 | Unsplash (random beautiful images) | **Widget System Features:** - Drag-and-drop grid layout (react-grid-layout) - Resizable widgets - Widget settings/configuration - Lazy loading for performance - Two-tier storage persistence (IndexedDB + localStorage) --- ### AI Terminal **Status:** Shipped (8 AI providers, 200+ models) | Provider | Models | Features | |----------|--------|----------| | OpenRouter | 200+ | Free tier (50 req/day) | | Groq | LPU-powered | Ultra-fast inference | | HuggingFace | Open-source | Community models | | Mistral | European | GDPR-friendly | | Google Gemini | 1M context | Long documents | | OpenAI | GPT-4o, o1 | Latest models | | Anthropic | Claude 3.5 | Sonnet & Opus | | xAI | Grok | Real-time info | | **DeepSeek** | R1, V3 | Cost-effective reasoning | **Security Features:** - AES-256-GCM encrypted API key storage - Session-based password with expiry - Keys never logged or transmitted - Automatic provider fallback - Usage tracking per provider **Phantom Shell (Browser Terminal):** - Full xterm.js terminal emulator - Node.js in browser via WebContainers - AI ghost commands with `/ai` prefix - Live preview for dev servers --- ## Privacy & Security ### Zero-Data-Host Architecture | What | Where | You Control? | |------|-------|--------------| | Notes | Your device (IndexedDB) | 100% | | Tasks | Your device (IndexedDB) | 100% | | Calendar | Your device (IndexedDB) | 100% | | Time Entries | Your device (IndexedDB) | 100% | | Settings | Your device (IndexedDB) | 100% | | API Keys | Your device (encrypted) | 100% | ### Privacy Guarantees 1. **No data EVER leaves your device** - Everything stays in IndexedDB (50GB+ capacity) 2. **Zero tracking** - No Google Analytics, no Facebook Pixel, no ads 3. **No account required** - Use anonymously with local storage 4. **No cloud dependencies** - No Firebase, no Google Drive, no third-party servers 5. **Full data export always available** - Export to .brain files anytime 6. **Open source** - Audit the code yourself ### Analytics (Optional) We use **Cloudflare Web Analytics** for basic, anonymous site metrics: - Privacy-focused (no cookies, no fingerprinting) - GDPR/CCPA compliant - IP addresses never stored - No individual user tracking **[Read full Privacy Policy](https://brain.neuman.dev)** (Click "Privacy & Terms" in the footer) --- ## Comparison with Alternatives | Feature | Neuman Brain | Notion | Obsidian | Monday.com | Toggl | |---------|--------------|--------|----------|------------|-------| | **Cost** | Free | $10-15/mo | Free (Sync $10) | $12-20/mo | Free-$18/mo | | **Data Privacy** | Local-first | Cloud-only | Local files | Cloud-only | Cloud-only | | **Platform** | Web, PWA | Web, apps | Desktop-first | Web, apps | Web, apps | | **Offline** | Full | Limited | Full | No | Limited | | **Notes + Tasks** | Integrated | Separate | Plugin-based | Weak notes | No notes | | **Time Tracking** | Built-in | No | Plugin | Partial | Core | | **Bundle Size** | 117KB | 2-4MB | Fast | 3MB+ | N/A | | **Open Source** | Yes (MIT) | No | Source-available | No | No | ### Feature Parity | Category | Competitor | Parity | |----------|------------|--------| | Notes | Obsidian | 55% | | Notes | Notion | 50% | | Tasks/PM | Jira/Linear | 98% | | Time Tracking | Toggl | 74% | | Calendar | Google Calendar | 65% | | Dashboard | Notion | 70% | --- ## Getting Started ### For Users **No installation required!** Just visit: **[https://brain.neuman.dev](https://brain.neuman.dev)** Your data is stored locally in your browser. No account needed. **Quick Start:** 1. Visit the app 2. Start creating notes, tasks, or events 3. Track your time with the sidebar timer or Timer tab 4. Explore the 44+ dashboard widgets 5. Export your data anytime (Settings → Backup & Sync) --- ### For Developers **Prerequisites:** - Node.js 22+ (see `.node-version`) - npm or pnpm **Setup:** ```bash # Clone repository git clone https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase.git cd system.knowledgebase # Install dependencies npm install # Start development server npm run dev # Open http://localhost:5173 # Run tests npm run test # Type checking npm run type-check # Build for production npm run build ``` **Developer Workflow:** We use a task-based workflow documented in `CLAUDE.md`: 1. **Before coding:** Create task file in `tasks/[feature-name].md` 2. **During work:** Update task file with progress 3. **Git hooks:** Pre-commit hook blocks commits without task files 4. **After work:** Add review section, archive completed tasks **Key Files:** - `CLAUDE.md` - Workflow guide with 12 core rules - `.claude/workflow-checklist.md` - Step-by-step checklist - `.claude/templates/task-template.md` - Task file template --- ## Architecture & Tech Stack ### Frontend | Technology | Version | Purpose | |------------|---------|---------| | React | 19 | UI framework | | TypeScript | 5.9 | Type safety (strict mode) | | Vite | 7 | Build tool, HMR | | Zustand | 5 | State management (1KB) | | Tailwind CSS | 3 | Styling | | Vitest | - | Testing | ### Data Storage (Local-First) | Layer | Technology | Capacity | |-------|------------|----------| | Primary | IndexedDB | 50GB+ | | Backups | .brain files | Unlimited | | Auto-save | File System Access API | User folder | ### Performance | Metric | Value | |--------|-------| | Bundle Size | 117KB gzipped | | Initial Load | < 1s | | Code Splitting | 9 feature-based chunks | | Lazy Loading | Widgets, Calendar, Kanban, AI | ### Project Structure ``` system.knowledgebase/ ├── src/ │ ├── pages/ # Dashboard, Tasks, Notes, TimeTracking, Settings │ ├── widgets/ # 44+ dashboard widgets │ ├── components/ # Reusable UI components │ ├── stores/ # Zustand state management │ ├── services/ # Data persistence, backup, AI │ └── types/ # TypeScript definitions ├── docs/ # Documentation (56 files) ├── tasks/ # Active development tasks ├── .archive/ # Completed tasks, historical docs └── .claude/ # Automation tools, templates ``` --- ## Roadmap ### Phase 0: Foundation ✅ Complete - React/TypeScript rebuild - Kanban, Calendar, Time Tracking - Privacy-first local storage - AI Terminal with 8+ providers ### Phase 1: Notes & PKM ✅ Complete - Lexical rich text editor - Folders, tags, search - Slash commands, markdown - Export to Markdown ### Phase 2: Stability & Polish (Current) - Modal overflow fixes - Timer Tab in Schedule page - Calendar day detail modal - Notes sidebar resize ### Phase 3: Habit Tracking & Gamification (Q1 2026) - Daily habits with streaks - XP, levels, badges - Analytics and visualizations ### Phase 4: Advanced PM (Q2 2026) - Gantt charts - Resource management - Billable time tracking ### Phase 5: Collaboration (Q2-Q3 2026) - Real-time sync - Comments & @mentions - Shared workspaces **[Full Roadmap](docs/product/feature_roadmap.md)** --- ## Contributing We welcome contributions! Neuman Brain is open source (MIT License). ### How to Contribute 1. **Report bugs:** [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues) 2. **Request features:** [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/discussions) 3. **Submit code:** Fork repo → Create feature branch → Submit PR 4. **Improve docs:** Documentation PRs are especially welcome! ### Development Guidelines - **TypeScript:** Strict mode, no `any` types - **Tests:** Add tests for new features (Vitest + Testing Library) - **Code Style:** Follow existing patterns, run `npm run lint` - **Documentation:** Update relevant docs in `/docs` - **Task Files:** Create task file before coding (see CLAUDE.md) **[See CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)** for detailed guidelines. --- ## Business Model ### How is this free? **No catch.** Neuman Brain has **zero hosting costs**: - Static hosting (Cloudflare Pages free tier) - No backend servers - No database hosting fees - Users store their own data locally ### Sustainability Plans | Model | Description | Status | |-------|-------------|--------| | Donations | GitHub Sponsors, Open Collective | Planned | | Client-side Pro | Advanced local features (your API key) | Future | | Professional Services | Custom integrations, training | Available | **Commitment:** Core functionality will **always be free**. **[Full Business Model](docs/business/business_model.md)** --- ## Legal ### Privacy Policy We respect your privacy. All data stays on YOUR device. We use Cloudflare Web Analytics (privacy-focused, no cookies) for basic site metrics. **[Read full Privacy Policy](https://brain.neuman.dev)** (Click "Privacy & Terms" in the footer) ### Terms & Conditions Currently being drafted. Core principles: - Your data, your control - Full data portability - No hidden agendas ### License **MIT License** - Use for anything, including commercial projects. See [LICENSE](LICENSE) for full terms. --- ## Acknowledgments ### Built With | Technology | Purpose | |------------|---------| | [React](https://react.dev/) | UI framework | | [TypeScript](https://www.typescriptlang.org/) | Type safety | | [Vite](https://vitejs.dev/) | Build tool | | [Zustand](https://github.com/pmndrs/zustand) | State management | | [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com/) | Styling | | [Lexical](https://lexical.dev/) | Rich text editor | | [Vitest](https://vitest.dev/) | Testing | | [Leaflet](https://leafletjs.com/) | Maps | | [Open-Meteo](https://open-meteo.com/) | Weather API | ### Inspired By - **Notion** - All-in-one flexibility - **Obsidian** - Local-first, powerful linking - **Apple Notes** - Simplicity and speed - **Linear** - Professional task management - **Toggl** - Elegant time tracking - **Habitica** - Gamification for productivity --- ## Contact | Channel | Link | |---------|------| | **Live App** | [brain.neuman.dev](https://brain.neuman.dev) | | **GitHub** | [github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase) | | **Issues** | [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues) | | **Discussions** | [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/discussions) | | **Email** | [brain@neuman.dev](mailto:brain@neuman.dev) | ---
### Star This Repo! If you find Neuman Brain useful, please star the repository on GitHub! It helps others discover the project. [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase?style=social)](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/stargazers) --- **Made with care for privacy, productivity, and open source** **Your data. Your device. Your brain.** --- *Last updated: November 26, 2025*

# Neuman Brain Documentation

**Last Updated:** November 26, 2025

This directory contains all documentation for the Neuman Brain project. Use this guide to navigate the documentation structure.

---

## Directory Structure

### Product & Strategy
| Directory | Purpose | Key Files |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| `/product/` | Feature specifications and roadmaps | `feature_map.md`, `feature_roadmap.md` |
| `/strategy/` | Business strategy and competitive analysis | `master_plan.md`, `next_steps_checklist.md` |
| `/business/` | Business model and go-to-market plans | `business_model.md`, `go_to_market.md` |
| `/overview/` | High-level system overview | `system_overview.md` |

### Technical Documentation
| Directory | Purpose | Key Files |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| `/technical/` | Architecture, implementation, guidelines | `development_principles.md`, `widget_development_guide.md` |
| `/security_privacy/` | Privacy model and compliance | `privacy_model.md`, `compliance.md` |

### User-Facing Documentation
| Directory | Purpose | Key Files |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| `/user/` | End-user feature guides | `ai_terminal_guide.md`, `terminal-complete-guide.md` |
| `/user-guide/` | Essential user guides (start here!) | `getting-started.md`, `notes.md`, `tasks.md`, `calendar.md`, `time-tracking.md`, `automation.md`, `troubleshooting.md` |
| `/user-guides/` | Comprehensive tutorials | `backup-sync-guide.md` |

### Brand & Design
| Directory | Purpose | Key Files |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| `/brand/` | Brand guidelines and visual standards | `brand_guide.md`, `visual_inspiration_and_standards.md` |

### Planning & Research
| Directory | Purpose | Key Files |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| `/plans/` | Strategic proposals (pre-implementation) | `README.md` explains lifecycle |
| `/research/` | Competitive analysis and market research | `time-tracking-competitive-analysis.md` |

### Session Records
| Directory | Purpose | Key Files |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| `/ai-sessions/` | AI session artifacts and reports | `README.md` explains purpose |
| `/gemini/` | Gemini-specific session outputs | `Gemini_Review_11222025.md` |
| `/reports/` | Formal review reports | Global review documents |

### Testing
| Directory | Purpose | Key Files |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| `/testing/` | Test plans and manual test guides | Phase-specific test guides |

---

## Quick Links

### For New Contributors
1. Start with [`/technical/development_principles.md`](./technical/development_principles.md)
2. Review [`/product/feature_map.md`](./product/feature_map.md) for current feature status
3. Check [`/technical/widget_development_guide.md`](./technical/widget_development_guide.md) if working on widgets

### For Product Understanding
1. [`/product/feature_roadmap.md`](./product/feature_roadmap.md) - Phased delivery plan
2. [`/strategy/master_plan.md`](./strategy/master_plan.md) - Strategic vision
3. [`CHANGELOG.md`](./CHANGELOG.md) - Release history

### For Users
1. **Start here**: [`/user-guide/getting-started.md`](./user-guide/getting-started.md) - First-time setup and essential information
2. **Feature Guides**:
   - [`/user-guide/notes.md`](./user-guide/notes.md) - Notes and knowledge management
   - [`/user-guide/tasks.md`](./user-guide/tasks.md) - Task and project management
   - [`/user-guide/calendar.md`](./user-guide/calendar.md) - Calendar and scheduling
   - [`/user-guide/time-tracking.md`](./user-guide/time-tracking.md) - Time tracking and productivity
   - [`/user-guide/automation.md`](./user-guide/automation.md) - Automation and workflows
3. **Get Help**: [`/user-guide/troubleshooting.md`](./user-guide/troubleshooting.md) - Common issues and solutions
4. **Advanced**:
   - [`/user/ai_terminal_guide.md`](./user/ai_terminal_guide.md) - AI Terminal usage
   - [`/user-guides/backup-sync-guide.md`](./user-guides/backup-sync-guide.md) - Backup and data management

---

## Workflow: Plans vs Tasks

**Strategic Proposals** (`/docs/plans/`)
- Long-term vision and proposals
- Reviewed before implementation
- Read-only reference during development

**Active Work** (`/tasks/` in root)
- Current implementation tasks
- Created from approved plans
- Archived when complete to `.archive/completed-tasks/`

**Flow:** Proposal in `/docs/plans/` -> Approved -> Task created in `/tasks/` -> Execution -> Archive

See [`/plans/README.md`](./plans/README.md) for detailed lifecycle documentation.

---

## Documentation Standards

1. **Last Updated Date**: Always include at top of file
2. **Purpose Statement**: Explain what the document covers
3. **Audience**: Who should read this document
4. **Status**: Active, Archived, Draft
5. **Links**: Cross-reference related documentation

---

## File Health Status

| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Active | Currently maintained and accurate |
| Stale | Needs review (>30 days old without updates) |
| Archived | Historical reference only |
| Draft | Work in progress |

---

**Maintained by:** Project Team
**Review Cycle:** Monthly audit of stale documentation

# Changelog

All notable changes to Neuman Brain will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

---

## [Unreleased]

### Added (November 26, 2025)
- **Time Tracking Timer Tab:**
  - Full-featured Timer tab in Schedule page (TimeTrackingTimer.tsx)
  - Synchronized with sidebar timer (shared Zustand store)
  - Weekly statistics chart with daily breakdown
  - Today/weekly/daily average displays
  - Recent entries grid with click-to-continue functionality
  - Matches all sidebar features but with enhanced UI

- **UI Polish (November 26, 2025):**
  - Modal overflow fixes (CardDetailPanel, QuickAddModal)
  - Event start date picker (EventCreateModal)
  - MonthlyTimeReport calendar unified with standard calendar style
  - Notes sidebar collapse/resize functionality restored

- **Foundation Stability (November 25, 2025):**
  - DayDetailModal for calendar day interactions
  - Kanban tasks displayed on calendar grid
  - ICS import date format normalization
  - StoreErrorBoundary for graceful error recovery
  - Data validation layer (Zod schemas)

### Planned
- Kanban PM improvements (custom columns, subtasks, comments)
- Dashboard drag-drop layout
- Settings & Integrations (Notion/Obsidian import)
- Cross-feature integration (link notes to tasks/events)
- End-to-end encryption (E2EE)

---

## [1.0.0] - 2025-11-18

### Summary
Sprint 7: Performance Optimization - 76% bundle reduction, 100x stronger encryption, 50-100x faster imports. Critical architectural improvements based on comprehensive platform review.

### Added
- **WebCrypto API Migration:**
  - Native browser cryptography (AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption)

1→# src/widgets/CLAUDE.md — Widget System
     2→
     3→## Scope
     4→Widget components under `src/widgets/`. Dashboard widgets live in `Dashboard/`, other feature widgets in their respective folders (Kanban, NotesEditor, etc.).
     5→
     6→## Widget Categories
     7→| Category | Examples |
     8→|----------|----------|
     9→| **core** | MyDayWidget, TasksSummaryWidget, RecentNotesWidget, FormWidget |
    10→| **productivity** | PomodoroWidget, QuickAddWidget, TasksQuickAddWidget, UpcomingEventsWidget |
    11→| **news** | HackerNewsWidget, ProductHuntWidget, AINewsWidget, DevToWidget, RedditWidget |
    12→| **finance** | CryptoWidget, CurrencyWidget, StockMarketWidget |
    13→| **fun** | JokeWidget, FactsWidget, QuoteWidget, BoredWidget, TypingTestWidget, MemoryGameWidget |
    14→| **visual** | ColorPaletteWidget, PixelArtWidget, UnsplashWidget, MotivationalWidget |
    15→| **dev** | GitHubWidget, GitHubTrendingWidget, AwesomeListsWidget, RepoStatsWidget, PackageStatsWidget |
    16→| **utility** | WeatherForecastWidget, WorldClockWidget, CalculatorWidget, UnitConverterWidget, QRCodeWidget, CountdownWidget, ClipboardWidget, IPInfoWidget, DictionaryWidget, WikipediaWidget, ShortcutsWidget, UptimeWidget, AirQualityWidget, AnalyticsWidget, TabManagerWidget |
    17→
    18→## Key Files
    19→- `Dashboard/BaseWidget.tsx` — Standard wrapper (loading/error/refresh states)
    20→- `Dashboard/WidgetRegistry.ts` — Widget discovery + metadata registry
    21→- `Kanban.tsx`, `NotesEditor.tsx`, `Weather.tsx`, `WeatherMap.tsx`, `Map.tsx` — Feature widgets
    22→
    23→## Non-negotiables
    24→- **Use `BaseWidget`:** All dashboard widgets must wrap in `BaseWidget` for consistent UX.
    25→- **Lazy loading:** Widgets discovered via Vite glob in `WidgetRegistry.ts`. Never import directly.
    26→- **Registration required:** Add to `WIDGET_FILE_NAMES` + `WIDGET_REGISTRY` with: `id`, `title`, `category`, `icon`, `description`.
    27→- **Semantic tokens:** Use Tailwind semantic classes. No hardcoded colors/sizes.
    28→- **Effect-based fetching:** Network calls in `useEffect` with cancellation handling.
    29→- **Accessibility:** ARIA labels, keyboard nav, visible focus.
    30→
    31→## Adding a Dashboard Widget
    32→
    33→**For complex widgets** (new category, multiple features, external API integration):
    34→1. Use **`/create-plan`** to design architecture before coding
    35→2. Plan should cover: component structure, data fetching, error handling, accessibility, bundle size
    36→
    37→**For standard widgets:**
    38→1. Create `Dashboard/MyWidget.tsx`:
    39→```tsx
    40→export default function MyWidget() {
    41→  const [data, setData] = useState<T | null>(null);
    42→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
    43→
    44→  useEffect(() => {
    45→    let cancelled = false;
    46→    async function load() {
    47→      try {
    48→        const result = await fetchData();
    49→        if (!cancelled) setData(result);
    50→      } catch (err) {
    51→        if (!cancelled) setError(String(err));
    52→      }
    53→    }
    54→    load();
    55→    return () => { cancelled = true; };
    56→  }, []);
    57→
    58→  if (error) return <ErrorMessage message={error} />;
    59→  if (!data) return <Loader />;
    60→
    61→  return (
    62→    <BaseWidget title="My Widget">
    63→      {/* Content */}
    64→    </BaseWidget>
    65→  );
    66→}
    67→```
    68→2. Add `'MyWidget'` to `WIDGET_FILE_NAMES` in `WidgetRegistry.ts`
    69→3. Add registry entry with metadata
    70→4. Run `npm run build` to verify bundle size
    71→5. For performance issues, use **`/consider:pareto`** to identify high-impact optimizations
    72→
    73→See [Tache Integration Guide § Widget Development](../../docs/technical/tache-integration-guide.md#workflow-widget-development) for complete workflow.
    74→
    75→## Anti-patterns
    76→- Registering widgets outside `WidgetRegistry.ts`.
    77→- Network calls in component body (use `useEffect`).
    78→- Hardcoded pixel sizes (use responsive units).
    79→- Heavy main-thread computation (offload to workers or memoize).
    80→- Global variables for state (use stores or local state).
    81→- Complex widget development without planning (use **`/create-plan`** for multi-phase widgets).
    82→- Over-engineering widget APIs (use **`/consider:via-negativa`** to simplify config).
    83→
    84→## Related
    85→- Widget guide: `docs/technical/widget_development_guide.md`
    86→- Stores: `src/stores/CLAUDE.md`
    87→

1→# Development Principles - Neuman Brain
     2→
     3→**Last Updated:** November 13, 2025  
     4→**Status:** 🔒 MANDATORY - All code must follow these principles  
     5→**Audience:** Developers, contributors, stakeholders
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Core Principles (Non-Negotiable)
    10→
    11→### 1. Privacy First 🔒
    12→
    13→**Every feature, every line of code, every decision must prioritize user privacy.**
    14→
    15→#### Mandatory Privacy Requirements:
    16→
    17→✅ **Local-first by default**
    18→- Data stays on user's device unless they explicitly choose cloud sync
    19→- No telemetry, analytics, or tracking without explicit opt-in
    20→- No data sent to Neuman Brain servers (zero-data-host architecture)
    21→
    22→✅ **User controls their data**
    23→- Users choose where data is stored (local folder, cloud provider, etc.)
    24→- Easy export at any time (no lock-in)
    25→- Clear communication about where data goes
    26→
    27→✅ **Encryption everywhere (Q2 2026+)**
    28→- End-to-end encryption for all backups (.brain files)
    29→- User-controlled passphrases (we never see them)
    30→- Zero-knowledge architecture (we can't read user data even if we wanted to)
    31→
    32→❌ **Never allowed:**
    33→- Uploading user data to our servers
    34→- Tracking individual user behavior
    35→- Selling data to third parties
    36→- Building features that require server-side access to content
    37→- Collecting PII without explicit consent
    38→
    39→#### Privacy Checklist for Every Feature:
    40→
    41→Before shipping ANY feature, verify:
    42→
    43→- [ ] Does this feature send data anywhere? (If yes, user must explicitly opt-in)
    44→- [ ] Can this feature work offline? (If not, why not?)
    45→- [ ] Is user data encrypted in transit and at rest?
    46→- [ ] Can users delete/export this data easily?
    47→- [ ] Have we documented privacy implications in user-facing language?
    48→- [ ] Does this respect GDPR/CCPA by default?
    49→
    50→---
    51→
    52→### 2. Security First 🛡️
    53→
    54→**Security is not a feature to add later - it's the foundation.**
    55→
    56→#### Mandatory Security Requirements:
    57→
    58→✅ **Secure by default**
    59→- All user inputs sanitized (XSS prevention)
    60→- Content Security Policy enforced
    61→- HTTPS only (no mixed content)
    62→- Secure random for crypto operations (crypto.getRandomValues)
    63→
    64→✅ **Use proven libraries**
    65→- WebCrypto API for encryption (not custom crypto!)
    66→- Established libraries for auth (if ever needed)
    67→- Regular dependency updates (npm audit weekly)
    68→
    69→✅ **Defense in depth**
    70→- Validate on client AND server (when applicable)
    71→- Principle of least privilege
    72→- Fail securely (errors don't leak sensitive info)
    73→
    74→❌ **Never allowed:**
    75→- Custom crypto implementations
    76→- Storing secrets in code (use env vars, user-controlled passphrases)
    77→- eval() or innerHTML with user content
    78→- Ignoring security warnings

# Development Principles - Neuman Brain

**Last Updated:** November 13, 2025  
**Status:** 🔒 MANDATORY - All code must follow these principles  
**Audience:** Developers, contributors, stakeholders

---

## Core Principles (Non-Negotiable)

### 1. Privacy First 🔒

**Every feature, every line of code, every decision must prioritize user privacy.**

#### Mandatory Privacy Requirements:

✅ **Local-first by default**
- Data stays on user's device unless they explicitly choose cloud sync
- No telemetry, analytics, or tracking without explicit opt-in
- No data sent to Neuman Brain servers (zero-data-host architecture)

✅ **User controls their data**
- Users choose where data is stored (local folder, cloud provider, etc.)
- Easy export at any time (no lock-in)
- Clear communication about where data goes

✅ **Encryption everywhere (Q2 2026+)**
- End-to-end encryption for all backups (.brain files)
- User-controlled passphrases (we never see them)
- Zero-knowledge architecture (we can't read user data even if we wanted to)

❌ **Never allowed:**
- Uploading user data to our servers
- Tracking individual user behavior
- Selling data to third parties
- Building features that require server-side access to content
- Collecting PII without explicit consent

#### Privacy Checklist for Every Feature:

Before shipping ANY feature, verify:

- [ ] Does this feature send data anywhere? (If yes, user must explicitly opt-in)
- [ ] Can this feature work offline? (If not, why not?)
- [ ] Is user data encrypted in transit and at rest?
- [ ] Can users delete/export this data easily?
- [ ] Have we documented privacy implications in user-facing language?
- [ ] Does this respect GDPR/CCPA by default?

---

### 2. Security First 🛡️

**Security is not a feature to add later - it's the foundation.**

#### Mandatory Security Requirements:

✅ **Secure by default**
- All user inputs sanitized (XSS prevention)
- Content Security Policy enforced
- HTTPS only (no mixed content)
- Secure random for crypto operations (crypto.getRandomValues)

✅ **Use proven libraries**
- WebCrypto API for encryption (not custom crypto!)
- Established libraries for auth (if ever needed)
- Regular dependency updates (npm audit weekly)

✅ **Defense in depth**
- Validate on client AND server (when applicable)
- Principle of least privilege
- Fail securely (errors don't leak sensitive info)

❌ **Never allowed:**
- Custom crypto implementations
- Storing secrets in code (use env vars, user-controlled passphrases)
- eval() or innerHTML with user content
- Ignoring security warnings
- Skipping security reviews for "speed"

#### Security Checklist for Every Feature:

Before shipping ANY feature, verify:

- [ ] All user inputs are sanitized
- [ ] No XSS vulnerabilities (run automated tests)
- [ ] No SQL injection (if database ever added)
- [ ] Dependencies are up-to-date (npm audit clean)
- [ ] Secrets are not in code or committed to git
- [ ] Error messages don't leak sensitive info
- [ ] Security-sensitive code has been reviewed

---

### 3. Beautiful UX First 🎨

**Privacy and security should feel effortless, not burdensome.**

    14→

    16→
    17→**Core Components:**
    18→- **`BaseWidget.tsx`** - Reusable wrapper providing loading states, error handling, refresh buttons, and consistent styling
    19→- **`WidgetRegistry.ts`** - Central registry mapping widget IDs to metadata, API configuration, and categories

    39→

    44→ *

    49→import { BaseWidget } from './BaseWidget';
    50→
    51→export const MyNewWidget: React.FC = () => {
    52→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
    53→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
    54→
    55→  const fetchData = useCallback(async () => {
    56→    setLoading(true);
    57→    setError(null);
    58→
    59→    try {
    60→      // Your data fetching logic here
    61→      setLoading(false);
    62→    } catch (err) {
    63→      setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to load data');
    64→      setLoading(false);
    65→    }
    66→  }, []);
    67→
    68→  // Initial fetch
    69→  useEffect(() => {
    70→    fetchData();
    71→  }, [fetchData]);
    72→
    73→  return (
    74→    <BaseWidget
    75→      title="My Widget"
    76→      icon="⚡"
    77→      loading={loading}
    78→      error={error}
    79→      onRefresh={fetchData}
    80→    >

# Taches-CC-Resources Integration Guide

## Introduction

### What is taches-cc-resources?

**taches-cc-resources** is a Claude Code plugin providing a comprehensive suite of productivity and development tools:

- **7 specialized skills** for creating plans, prompts, hooks, and debugging complex issues
- **18 slash commands** for invoking workflows, managing tasks, and auditing quality
- **12 thinking frameworks** (`/consider:*` commands) for structured decision-making
- **3 subagents** for auditing and quality assurance

Think of it as a **second brain for solo development**—automating the meta-work of planning, organizing, delegating, and thinking systematically.

### Why Integrate It Into Neuman Brain?

Neuman Brain is solving a **hard problem**: building a privacy-first, local-only productivity dashboard with complex features (widgets, automation, AI, offline sync). This requires:

1. **Systematic planning** for multi-phase features (Graph View, Offline Mode, AI Integration)
2. **Smart delegation** when context gets heavy (research → plan → implement)
3. **Structured decision-making** for architectural choices (which storage? state management? performance tradeoffs?)
4. **Root cause debugging** for intermittent bugs (IndexedDB, Zustand persistence, race conditions)
5. **Quality assurance** before committing code (skills, commands, agents)
6. **Context hygiene** across long development sessions (task handoff, `/clear`, resumption)

**taches-cc-resources bridges these gaps**, turning ad-hoc development into systematic, repeatable workflows.

### How This Guide Is Organized

- **Part 1: Quick Start** — Get productive immediately with the intelligent routing system and 20% of features you'll use 80% of the time
- **Part 2: Intelligent Routing System (CRITICAL)** — Automatic prompt analysis and feature routing (the core innovation)
- **Part 3: Feature Deep Dive** — Detailed reference for all skills, commands, and frameworks
- **Part 4: Workflows** — Real-world development scenarios mapped to tache features
- **Part 5: Best Practices** — Conventions, pitfalls, advanced techniques
- **Part 6: Reference** — Quick lookup tables and decision trees

---

## Part 1: Quick Start

### The Intelligent Routing System

**What it does:** Automatically analyzes any prompt you write and routes it through the optimal tache feature(s)—no manual decision-making required.

**Example:**
```
You: "Debug why IndexedDB fails intermittently in the time tracking store"
System analysis:
  - Keywords: "debug", "intermittently", "why" → suggests debug-like-expert
  - Complexity: Complex, intermittent → suggests systematic investigation
  - Type: Debugging → confirms /debug or debug-like-expert
Automatic route: debug-like-expert skill (for systematic root cause analysis)
```

**Another example:**
```
You: "Plan the implementation of the Graph View feature with D3 integration"
System analysis:
  - Keywords: "plan", "implementation" → suggests /create-plan
  - Scope: Multi-phase feature → supports /create-plan
  - Complexity: Moderate-high → supports hierarchical planning
Automatic route: /create-plan (creates phases, tasks, milestones)
```

**How it works (3 steps):**
1. **Analyze**: Extract keywords, complexity, scope, type from your prompt
2. **Match**: Apply routing rules to find best-fit feature(s)
3. **Route**: Execute feature, pass context, return results

**Default behavior**: Routing happens automatically. You type what you need; the system figures out the best workflow. No special syntax required.

---

### Your First Tache Workflow

**Scenario:** Adding a new analytics dashboard widget

**Step 1: Planning**
```
You: "Plan the analytics widget with real-time data visualization"
→ Automatic route: /create-plan
→ System creates hierarchical plan with phases, tasks, verification criteria
→ You review plan (5 min)
```

**Step 2: Approval Gate**
```
Plan created. Review and use /run-plan to execute when ready.
→ You approve by running /run-plan
```

**Step 3: Execution with Tracking**
```
You: "/run-plan .planning/phases/[number]-analytics-widget/PLAN.md"
→ Plan executes step-by-step
→ Track progress with /check-todos (optional)
```

**Step 4: Quality Assurance (if creating skills/commands)**

# Master Plan: Neuman Brain Dashboard (2026-2027)

**Last Updated:** November 19, 2025
**Status:** ✅ Active
**Purpose:** Unified strategic roadmap consolidating product vision, technical execution, and quality standards

---

## Executive Summary

**Vision:** Build the world's most privacy-respecting, performance-obsessed personal knowledge management (PKM) and productivity platform.

**Current State (Nov 19, 2025):**
- **Codebase Health:** Zero TypeScript errors, zero ESLint errors (production code)
- **Bundle Size:** 115KB gzipped initial load (15KB over 100KB target - acceptable)
- **Feature Completeness:** 8 major features shipped, Sprint 8 (Codebase Audit) complete
- **Quality Bar:** Production-ready code, comprehensive documentation (26 docs)

**Strategic Positioning:**
- **vs Notion:** 10x faster (115KB vs 1.2MB), privacy-first, free forever
- **vs Obsidian:** Web-based, no plugins needed, beautiful UX out-of-the-box
- **vs Linear/Trello:** Integrated PKM + PM, offline-first, zero vendor lock-in

---

## I. Foundation Principles

### 1. Privacy First (Zero-Data-Host Architecture)

**Non-negotiable:**
- All data stays on user's device (IndexedDB - 50GB+ capacity)
- No server storage, no tracking, no analytics (without opt-in)
- AES-256-GCM encryption for API keys (WebCrypto API)
- E2EE backups (Q2 2026)

**Competitive Advantage:**
- Users own their data (exportable .brain files)
- Works offline by default
- No privacy policy concerns (no data collection)

### 2. Performance Obsession

**Targets:**
- Bundle size: < 100KB initial load (current: 115KB - 15KB over but feature-rich)
- Load time: < 1s on 3G connection
- Interaction latency: < 100ms for all UI actions
- Animations: 60fps always

**Current Performance:**
```
Initial Load: 115KB gzipped
- index.js: 43KB
- react-vendor.js: 61KB
- router-vendor.js: 12KB

Lazy Chunks (loaded on demand):
- lexical-vendor.js: 79KB (Notes page only)
- ai-vendor.js: 45KB (AI Terminal only)
- notes.js: 9.6KB
```


**How We Stay Fast:**
- Aggressive code splitting (vite.config.ts manualChunks)
- Tree shaking (named imports only)
- Lazy loading (React.lazy for Settings, Notes, Planning pages)
- No heavyweight dependencies (chose Zustand over Redux)

---

## II. Current State (Nov 19, 2025)

### Sprint 8: Codebase Audit - COMPLETE (9h)
- Validated all Gemini documentation (100% accurate)
- Fixed 3 critical bugs
- Zero TypeScript/ESLint errors
- Bundle: 586KB to 115KB (80% reduction)
- Created AI Provider + Widget Development guides

### Feature Parity

| Feature | vs Competitor | Parity | Status |
|---------|--------------|--------|--------|
| Notes | Obsidian | 55% | Production |
| Calendar | Google Calendar | 65% | Production |
| Kanban | Trello/Linear | 65%/45% | Production |
| Dashboard | Notion | 70% | Production |
| AI Terminal | Custom | 100% | Production |

---

## III. 2026-2027 Roadmap

### Q1 2026: Foundation (COMPLETE)
- Sprint 8 complete
- Zero errors, comprehensive docs
- 115KB bundle

### Q1-Q2 2026: 20-Week Improvement Plan
- Phase 4: Kanban to PM Powerhouse

# Competitive Landscape Analysis

**Last Updated:** December 4, 2025
**Purpose:** Comprehensive competitive analysis across 6+ product categories
**Audience:** Product, marketing, business strategy
**Scope:** 50+ competitors analyzed

---

## Executive Summary

Neuman Brain operates in a highly competitive $96.36B productivity software market, competing across **6 overlapping categories** with 50+ established players. Our unique positioning combines features from multiple categories into a single free, privacy-first, local-first platform.

**Key Finding:** No competitor combines PM tools + habit tracking + gamification while remaining 100% free and privacy-first. This is our blue ocean opportunity.

### Market Gap Identified
- **Cost Advantage:** Average competitor costs $500-2,000/year; we're $0
- **Speed Advantage:** 60KB bundle vs 2-4MB (10x-40x faster)
- **Privacy Advantage:** Local-first by default vs cloud-only
- **Feature Combination:** Only platform combining professional PM + habit tracking + gamification

---

## Product Categories

Neuman Brain competes in 6 overlapping categories:

1. **Project/Task Management** (Monday.com, ClickUp, Asana, Trello, Jira)
2. **PKM/Notes** (Notion, Obsidian, Logseq, Anytype)
3. **Daily Planning** (Sunsama, Akiflow, Motion)
4. **Time Tracking** (Toggl, Clockify, Harvest)
5. **IT Helpdesk/Ticketing** (Jira Service Desk, Zendesk, Freshdesk)
6. **Habit Tracking** (Habitica, Streaks, Todoist, TickTick)

---

## Category 1: Project/Task Management

### Monday.com
- **Pricing:** $12-20/user/month ($144-240/year for 10 people)
- **Strengths:** Visual workflows, automations, integrations
- **Weaknesses:** Expensive, complex UI, cloud-only
- **Our Advantage:** Free, 10x faster, local-first

### ClickUp
- **Pricing:** $9-19/user/month
- **Strengths:** Feature-rich, customizable
- **Weaknesses:** Overwhelming UI, slow performance, privacy concerns
- **Our Advantage:** Simpler, faster, privacy-first

### Asana
- **Pricing:** $13.49-30.49/user/month
- **Strengths:** Clean UI, solid collaboration
- **Weaknesses:** Limited free tier, expensive for teams
- **Our Advantage:** Free, open source, local-first

### Todoist
- **Pricing:** $5/month ($48/year)
- **Strengths:** Simple, fast, good mobile apps
- **Weaknesses:** No projects/time tracking, limited free tier
- **Our Advantage:** More features (time tracking, projects), free

### Fizzy (fizzy.do)
- **Pricing:** Free for 1,000 cards (lifetime), $20/month unlimited
- **Strengths:** 37signals pedigree (Basecamp makers), vibrant UI, keyboard-first (Do Bar), open source (O'Saasy License), self-hostable, fast, Rails + Hotwire
- **Weaknesses:** Kanban-only (no notes/time tracking), O'Saasy license prevents SaaS competition, Rails stack (less portable)
- **Our Advantage:** Full productivity suite (notes, time tracking, calendar), MIT license (true open source), JavaScript/TypeScript stack, more comprehensive feature set
- **Notable:** Golden Ticket priority system, command-line style UI, anti-muted design philosophy, 3K+ GitHub stars (Dec 2025)

---

## Category 2: PKM/Notes

### Notion
- **Pricing:** $10-18/user/month
- **Strengths:** Flexible databases, beautiful UI, collaboration
- **Weaknesses:** Slow (2MB bundle), cloud-only, privacy concerns, $120-216/year

# Neuman Brain - System Overview

**Version:** 2.0.0  
**Last Updated:** November 13, 2025  
**Audience:** Engineers, Product Managers, Contributors

---

## What is Neuman Brain?

Neuman Brain is a **privacy-first, local-first productivity platform** combining notes, tasks, projects, and time tracking in a single React-based web application. It positions itself as a free alternative to enterprise tools like Monday.com, Notion, and ClickUp, with planned gamification features inspired by Habitica.

### Current Reality (v2.0.0)

Neuman Brain is a **React 19/TypeScript 5.9 Single-Page Application (SPA)** providing:

1. **Task Management (Kanban)** - Drag-and-drop board, rich metadata, keyboard shortcuts
2. **Calendar Management** - Monthly/weekly views, syncs with tasks
3. **Time Tracking** - Toggl-style time entries (store ready, UI partial)
4. **Environmental Widgets** - Weather + Map with bidirectional sync
5. **Project Management Infrastructure** - Types/stores defined for Gantt, resources, risks (UI not built)
6. **System Monitoring** - Types defined for IT/sysadmin features (future)

### **CRITICAL GAP: NO NOTES/PKM**

Despite requirements that "Notes & Knowledge Management must be a first-class citizen competitive with Apple Notes," the current codebase has **ZERO** notes/PKM features:

- ❌ No note types or data models
- ❌ No note-taking UI
- ❌ No markdown editor
- ❌ No organization (folders, tags, links)
- ❌ No search within notes

**This is the #1 priority for Q1 2026.**

---

## High-Level Architecture

### Technology Stack

**Frontend:**
- React 19.2 (UI framework)
- TypeScript 5.9 (type safety)
- Vite 7.2 (build tool - extremely fast)
- Zustand 5.0 (state management - 1KB)
- Tailwind CSS 3.4 (utility-first styling)
- @dnd-kit 6.3 (drag-and-drop)
- Leaflet 1.9 (maps)

**Data Storage (Multi-Tier):**
- **LocalStorage** (primary, 5-10MB limit)
- **IndexedDB** (available, 50GB+, underutilized)
- **Firebase** (optional cloud sync - user's project)
- **Google Drive** (optional backup - user's account)

**Testing:** Vitest 4.0 + @testing-library/react (71 passing tests)

**Deployment:** Cloudflare Pages (static hosting, $0 cost)

**Bundle Size:** 59.97 KB gzipped

---

## Major Components & Subsystems

### 1. Task & Project Management ✅ Implemented
- Kanban board with drag-drop
- Task CRUD with priorities, tags, dates, dependencies
- Calendar synchronization
- **Files:** `src/widgets/Kanban.tsx`, `src/stores/useKanbanStore.ts`

### 2. Calendar & Scheduling ✅ Implemented
- Monthly/weekly views
- Multiple events per day
- Task date range visualization
- **Files:** `src/widgets/Calendar.tsx`, `src/stores/useCalendarStore.ts`

### 3. Time Tracking ⚠️ Partially Implemented
- Store + DB ready, UI incomplete

**Last Updated:** November 19, 2025
**Status:** ✅ Active
**Source:** Validated from codebase audit + Gemini analysis

---

## Overview

This guide outlines the standard procedure for integrating new AI providers into the Neuman Brain ecosystem. Our architecture uses a unified `AIProvider` interface to ensure consistency, security, and error handling across all AI services.

## Current Architecture

**Location:** `src/services/ai/`

**Core Files:**

# Getting Started with Neuman Brain

Welcome to Neuman Brain! This guide will help you get up and running with your privacy-first productivity platform.

## What is Neuman Brain?

Neuman Brain is a local-first productivity platform that combines notes, tasks, calendar, time tracking, and more - all in one place, all stored on your device. Your data never leaves your computer unless you choose to export it.

## First Time Setup

### 1. Access the Platform

Simply visit [https://brain.neuman.dev](https://brain.neuman.dev) in your web browser. No account creation required!

The platform works in any modern browser:
- Chrome/Edge (recommended for best performance)
- Firefox
- Safari
- Opera

### 2. Understanding Local Storage

All your data is stored in your browser's local storage. This means:
- Your information stays on your device
- No server uploads or downloads
- Complete privacy by default
- Works offline once loaded

**Important**: Your data is tied to your browser. If you clear browser data or uninstall your browser, you'll lose your information unless you've created backups.

### 3. The Interface Tour

When you first open Neuman Brain, you'll see:

**Left Sidebar**: Navigation to all major features
- Dashboard (home page with widgets)
- Notes (capture thoughts and ideas)
- Tasks (project and task management)
- Calendar (events and scheduling)
- Time Tracking (track your work hours)
- Forms (create and fill out custom forms)
- Diagrams (visual diagrams and flowcharts)
- Graph View (visualize connections between notes)
- Link Library (bookmark management)
- Automations (automate repetitive actions)
- Settings (customize your experience)

**Main Area**: Your active workspace (Dashboard, Notes editor, Kanban board, etc.)

**Top Right**: Quick actions and view controls

### 4. Start Creating Content

The best way to learn is by creating:

1. **Create your first note**: Click "Notes" in the sidebar, then "+ New Note"
2. **Add a task**: Click "Tasks" in the sidebar, then "+ Add Task" in any column
3. **Schedule an event**: Click "Calendar" and click any day to create an event
4. **Start tracking time**: Click the timer icon in the right sidebar

## Understanding Your Data

### Where Is My Data Stored?

Your data is stored in your browser's IndexedDB - a powerful local database that can hold 50GB+ of information. Think of it like a filing cabinet inside your browser.

### How Neuman Brain Respects Your Privacy

1. **No Account Required**: Start using immediately without creating an account
2. **No Cloud Sync**: Your data never touches our servers
3. **No Tracking**: No Google Analytics, no Facebook Pixel, no ads
4. **No Third Parties**: No data shared with anyone
5. **Open Source**: You can review the code yourself on GitHub

The only network requests made are:
- Loading the app itself (HTML, CSS, JavaScript)
- Optional widget data (weather, news, crypto prices) - only if you enable those widgets
- AI features - only if you configure them and provide your own API keys

## Backup Your Data (Critical!)
